appRoles.description: May queue assessments and retests and prepare the assistant's confirmation-bound synthetic action; cannot record reviewer or risk-approval decisions.
May append reviewer decisions and remediation records; cannot accept risk or approve an exception unless separately assigned RiskApprover.
May append risk-treatment and exception decisions and reviewer decisions; does not grant Azure resource-plane authority.
Allows only the collector managed identity to obtain a Console-audience token for the expected Easy Auth 403 negative-authorization probe; grants no API command.

personaAssignments.use: append a reviewer decision or remediation
approve an exception or risk decision

workloadAssignments.use: mint a Console-audience token that Easy Auth deliberately rejects with 403

applications.roleClaimConsumer: The assurance API consumes the roles claim from X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL and denies commands by default.
applications.runtimeSurface: private Assurance Console and assurance API

appRoles.description: May use the assistant and prepare its confirmation-bound synthetic action; no assistant action can record an assurance conclusion.
May authenticate for policy lookup but receives no consequential assistant-tool permission from this role.
May authenticate for policy lookup but receives no consequential assistant-tool permission from this role.
Allows the assistant and collector managed identities to obtain an assistant-audience token for the identity-restricted Phi inference endpoint; grants no human assistant tool permission.

personaAssignments.use: policy lookup and confirmation-bound synthetic action

workloadAssignments.use: mint an assistant-audience token for the Phi inference endpoint
mint an assistant-audience token for live behavioral collection against Phi

applications.roleClaimConsumer: The assistant API consumes the roles claim from X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL; only Assurance.Assessor can prepare or execute the synthetic consequential tool.

credentialPolicy.note: The checked-in Container Apps Easy Auth configuration uses the ID-token-only browser flow and has no client-secret setting. Any future confidential-client flow requires a separately reviewed Key Vault-backed design.

requiredEvidence: foundation deployment output digest containing both callback URIs
application and service-principal object IDs and application client IDs
exact manifest/app-role readback for both registrations
appRoleAssignmentRequired readback for both service principals
role-assignment readback with principal identifiers retained only as private evidence
negative authorization probes for no role and wrong role
